title: campanula cantabrica
intro: Campanula cantabrica, also known as Cantabric bellflower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Campanulaceae family, characterized by its bell-shaped flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can be used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of Campanula cantabrica include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and aid in digestion. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in the form of essential oils and dried flowers for medicinal purposes.
